payload.text: I’ll trace the repository chat send path from the named IPC/service files into the frontend entry point, then patch the smallest lifecycle gap and add a regression around the failure path.

`rg` is not installed in this environment, so I’m falling back to targeted `grep`/file reads. The repo already has unrelated local changes, including the Codex service and backend tests, so I’ll avoid broad rewrites and patch only the repository chat behavior I can attribute to this ticket.

The frontend panel already clears `pending` in a `finally` path for normal rejections, so the likely remaining hang is a backend promise that never settles. I’m going to add a repository-chat-only backend timeout/race that aborts the Codex turn and rejects with a concise error, then cover that with a regression test.

I’m editing the Codex service only, adding a repository-chat-specific timeout dependency so tests can force the previously hanging path without touching ticket drafting or implementation run behavior.

The service now races the SDK turn against an aborting timeout and still preserves existing rejected/empty-result handling. I’m adding a focused backend regression where the fake Codex run never resolves, verifying the method rejects and aborts instead of remaining pending.
The regression is in place. I’m checking the test runner shape first so I can run the requested backend test target and the nearest repository chat UI test without guessing.

The test runner bundles all test entry points, so the backend and repository-chat UI coverage run through `npm test`. I’m running that now; if it exposes type or unrelated in-flight failures, I’ll separate what belongs to this fix from existing worktree state.

`npm test` passed, including the new timeout regression and the existing repository chat panel content tests. I’m running the TypeScript check as a final guard because the change adds dependency fields and a small generic helper.

> relay@0.1.0 typecheck
> tsc --noEmit

Implemented the repository chat hang fix.

**Summary**
Added a repository-chat-only Codex turn timeout in [src/main/services/codex/index.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/main/services/codex/index.ts:603). If `thread.run(...)` never settles, the service now aborts the turn and rejects with a handled error, so the existing frontend `.catch(...).finally(...)` path can show the inline error and clear loading.

Added a regression test in [tests/backend.test.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/backend.test.ts:449) that simulates a Codex repository chat run that never resolves and asserts it rejects promptly and aborts the signal.

**Files changed**
- [src/main/services/codex/index.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/main/services/codex/index.ts:603)
- [tests/backend.test.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/backend.test.ts:449)

**Commands run**
- `git status --short`
- `grep` / `sed` / `nl` code inspection commands
- `npm test`
- `npm run typecheck`

**Tests run**
- `npm test`: passed, 149 tests.
- `npm run typecheck`: passed.
- Manual validation in the running Electron UI was not performed.

**Subagent usage**
none used

**Remaining risks / follow-up**
The repo had many pre-existing unrelated local changes before this work, including in `src/main/services/codex/index.ts` and `tests/backend.test.ts`; I left those intact. The timeout is currently 120 seconds for real repository chat turns, with a test-only override.

Implemented the repository chat hang fix.

**Summary**
Added a repository-chat-only Codex turn timeout in [src/main/services/codex/index.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/main/services/codex/index.ts:603). If `thread.run(...)` never settles, the service now aborts the turn and rejects with a handled error, so the existing frontend `.catch(...).finally(...)` path can show the inline error and clear loading.

Added a regression test in [tests/backend.test.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/backend.test.ts:449) that simulates a Codex repository chat run that never resolves and asserts it rejects promptly and aborts the signal.
